description: Swingtrader monorepo dependency graph. Auto-generated by analyze_deps.py — do not edit manually.

how_to_read.nodes: py_module = file; py_func = individual function; db_table/view = DB object; external_api = HTTP endpoint.
how_to_read.edges: reads/writes/upserts = DB ops; fetches = HTTP; calls = function→function.
how_to_read.db_cross_reference: Inverted index: for each DB object, which functions/files access it.
how_to_read.function_call_graph: Which functions call which other functions (cross-file resolved via imports).

nodes.label: chat
nodes.description:
Send a chat request to the Anthropic API.

Parameters
----------
prompt  : user message content
system  : system message content
model   : model ID; defaults to ANTHROPIC_IMPACT_MODEL env var → claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
timeout : seconds before the request times out

Returns
-------
(response_text, latency_ms)

Raises
------
AnthropicError  if the API call fails or the response is malformed

nodes.label: _assistant_text
nodes.description:
Qwen3 / reasoning models may use ``content`` for JSON, ``reasoning_content`` for
chain-of-thought, or truncate ``content`` mid-JSON when ``max_tokens`` is hit.

Prefer the shortest field (or combination) that contains a **complete** JSON
object; otherwise concatenate both so downstream regex/partial salvage can run.

nodes.label: _get
nodes.description:
Single async GET with global rate limiting and 429 retry/backoff.

A global asyncio.Lock serialises request dispatch so all concurrent
FMPFetcher calls share one _DELAY-second gap between sends.
On 429 the request is retried up to _MAX_RETRIES times with
increasing back-off delays.

When ``expect_sparse_misses`` is True (sparse-fill random page probes),
empty responses and client errors (4xx) are logged at DEBUG instead of
WARNING because misses are expected.

nodes.label: fetch_all
nodes.description:
Fetch all endpoints sequentially (rate-limit friendly) and return
a RawCompanyData dataclass.  Fields with missing data are set to
empty list / empty dict rather than None so callers can safely iterate.

Override the LLM backend for this process (``ollama``, ``anthropic``, or ``do_agent``).

Updates ``os.environ["NEWS_IMPACT_BACKEND"]`` so downstream code agrees.
Used by ``score_news_cli --news-impact-backend``.

nodes.label: _chat

nodes.label: _extract_json_object
nodes.description:
Best-effort normalization for LLM JSON responses:
- remove markdown fences
- strip illegal leading '+' on JSON numbers
- isolate the first balanced ``{...}`` object (skips reasoning preamble before JSON)
- else fall back to substring from first ``{`` through last ``}``

nodes.label: _parse_head_response
nodes.description:
Parse JSON from LLM response.  Strips markdown fences, validates keys/ranges.
Returns (scores, reasoning, confidence).

If the full JSON is malformed (e.g. truncated mid-reasoning), falls back to
regex extraction of the scores block so partial results are not lost.

nodes.label: _run_head
nodes.description: Run a single cluster head, respecting the global concurrency semaphore.

nodes.label: _parse_relationship_response
nodes.description:
Parse the relationship head response.
Returns (scores, reasoning, confidence) where scores uses composite keys:
  "<FROM>__<TO>__<type>" → strength (float)
and reasoning uses the same keys → notes (str).

nodes.label: _run_relationship_head
nodes.description: Run the ticker-relationship head. Uses its own prompt and parser.

nodes.label: _parse_sentiment_response
nodes.description:
Parse the ticker-sentiment head response.
Returns (scores, reasoning, confidence) where scores = {ticker: sentiment_float}.

nodes.label: _run_sentiment_head
nodes.description: Run the per-ticker sentiment head.

nodes.label: extract_tickers
nodes.description:
Use the LLM to extract US ticker symbols mentioned in the article.
Returns a (possibly empty) list of uppercase ticker strings.
Never raises — returns [] on any failure.

nodes.label: score_article
nodes.description:
Run all cluster heads plus the ticker-relationship head in parallel.

Always returns one HeadOutput per cluster (including TICKER_RELATIONSHIPS).
Failed heads have error set and confidence=0.0 with empty scores.

nodes.label: _demo
nodes.description: def _demo in code/analytics/news_impact/impact_scorer.py:727

nodes.label: _fetch_relationship_edges
nodes.description:
Load canonicalized graph edges from ticker_relationship_network_resolved_v.
Mirrors UI relationshipsGetNeighborhood() data source + filters.

nodes.label: _resolve_canonical_tickers
nodes.description: Resolve ticker aliases to canonical tickers like the UI does.

nodes.label: _expand_related_tickers
nodes.description:
Multi-hop traversal (up to K steps) with per-hop decay.
Returns (ticker_score_map, ticker_paths_map).

nodes.label: _fetch_open_positions
nodes.description:
Compute net open position per ticker from user_trades.
Net long  = SUM(buy×long qty) - SUM(sell×long qty)  > 0
Net short = SUM(sell×short qty) - SUM(buy×short qty) > 0 (returned as negative)

nodes.label: _fetch_active_screen_tickers
nodes.description:
Return active screening tickers from the user's latest screening run only.

This avoids mixing stale active notes from older runs into today's narrative.

nodes.label: _fetch_ticker_aliases
nodes.description:
Return a map of {alias_norm → canonical_ticker} for all known aliases of the
given canonical tickers, using the same security_identity_map table as
_resolve_canonical_tickers (i.e. the same method the UI uses).
The canonical tickers themselves are included so the query covers everything.

nodes.label: _fetch_ticker_news
nodes.description:
For each ticker, find recent articles and the TICKER_SENTIMENT score.
Also pulls TICKER_RELATIONSHIPS data for relationship insights.
Articles tagged under any known alias of a ticker are included and mapped
back to the canonical ticker (same alias table the UI uses).
Returns {ticker: [TickerNewsItem, ...]}

nodes.label: _fetch_related_news_from_relationship_edges
nodes.description:
Pull related news directly from TICKER_RELATIONSHIPS heads for the constructed network.
This captures edge evidence even when news_article_tickers lacks certain entities (e.g. OPENAI).

nodes.label: _fetch_alert_items
nodes.description: Load active alerts and try to enrich with latest price from scan_rows.

nodes.label: _fetch_opted_in_users
nodes.description:
Return (user_id, lookback_hours) for all users where
narrative_preferences.is_enabled = TRUE.
Falls back to all users who have trades or active notes if no preferences exist.

nodes.label: _build_news_block
nodes.description: def _build_news_block in code/analytics/news_impact/narrative_generator.py:958

nodes.label: _build_related_news_block
nodes.description: def _build_related_news_block in code/analytics/news_impact/narrative_generator.py:996

nodes.label: _enrich_narrative_sources
nodes.description: Replace model article_id citations with title/url from DB; drop unknown ids. Mutates narrative.

nodes.label: _parse_narrative_json
nodes.description: Best-effort JSON extraction from Ollama response.

nodes.label: _call_section
nodes.description:
Call Ollama for one focused section. Returns (parsed_dict, latency_ms).
On any error returns ({}, 0) so the caller can fall back gracefully.

nodes.label: _generate_narrative_text
nodes.description:
Generate narrative by making 5 sequential focused Ollama calls instead of
one giant prompt. Each section call only receives the data it needs, keeping
prompts small and avoiding Ollama timeouts.

  1. portfolio_watch    — positions + portfolio news
  2. screening_update   — screening tickers + screening news
  3. related_network_update — related network news
  4. alert_watch        — active alerts + alert-ticker news
  5. market_pulse       — orchestrator using outputs of 1-4 + semantic evidence

Returns (parsed_narrative_dict, total_latency_ms).

nodes.label: _save_narrative
nodes.description: Upsert the daily narrative for this user+date.

nodes.label: _build_user_context
nodes.description:
Fetch all DB/embedding data for one user and return a populated UserContext.
Does NOT call Ollama — safe to call for dry-run / inspection.

nodes.label: build_prompt_for_user
nodes.description:
Build and return all section prompts for one user without calling the model.
Useful for dry-run inspection and input validation.

Returns (ctx, {section_name: prompt_string}).

nodes.label: generate_for_user
nodes.description:
Generate and persist the daily narrative for one user.
Returns the saved narrative dict.

nodes.label: generate_all
nodes.description:
Generate narratives for all opted-in users (sequentially — Ollama is single-GPU).
Returns (processed_user_ids, failed_user_ids).

nodes.label: _impact_for_article_id

nodes.description: Return article id if a row exists with this exact url string.

nodes.label: _check_existing
nodes.description:
Returns (article_id, impact_vector) if the article is already in the DB.

When ``url`` is non-empty, matches on normalized URL first (and legacy raw URL),
then falls back to ``article_hash``.

nodes.label: _delete_heads_and_vector
nodes.description: Remove existing heads and vector rows for an article (used before refresh).

nodes.label: _persist
nodes.description:
Insert (or re-use) article row, then insert heads and vector.
Returns article_id.

If existing_article_id is provided, the article row already exists and
we only write new heads/vector rows (caller must have deleted the old ones).

nodes.label: ingest_article
nodes.description:
Full pipeline: article → 8 LLM heads → aggregate → persist → return
(article_id, impact_vector).

Deduplicates by normalized URL when present, otherwise sha256(body).
If the article already exists and refresh=False, returns the cached result
without LLM calls. If refresh=True, re-scores and overwrites the stored heads and vector.

When ``page``/``limit``/dates are None, uses ``args.page``, ``args.limit``, ``args.from_date``, ``args.to_date``.

misses are logged at DEBUG in ``FMPFetcher`` instead of WARNING — expected noise.

nodes.label: _rescore_fetch_unscored_ids
nodes.description:
Equivalent to:
  SELECT article_id FROM news_impact_heads
  WHERE scores_json = '{}'
  GROUP BY article_id HAVING count(*) = 11
PostgREST groups server-side via count() — one row per article_id.
Covers articles that predate the processing_status column (NULL status).

nodes.label: _rescore_fetch_incomplete_ids

Find articles with processing_status = 'failed' — all heads failed, worth retrying
after an API key fix or outage recovery.

nodes.label: fetch_stock_posts
nodes.description:
Search all X posts (full archive) mentioning the given stock tickers and/or accounts.

Parameters
----------
tickers     : list of ticker symbols (e.g. ["AAPL", "MSFT"]), or None if only filtering by account
max_results : max posts to return across all tickers (up to 100 per API page)
accounts    : list of X account names (e.g. ["realDonaldTrump", "elonmusk"])

At least one of ``tickers`` or ``accounts`` must be provided.

Returns
-------
List of dicts with keys: symbol, title, text, url, published_at,
publisher, public_metrics.  Empty list on failure or missing token.

nodes.label: get_cluster_trends
nodes.description:
Latest cluster-level sentiment scores from news_trends_cluster_daily_v.

Returns one row per cluster per day, most recent first.
Columns: cluster_id, bucket_day, cluster_avg, cluster_weighted_avg,
         bucket_article_count, article_count.

nodes.label: get_dimension_trends
nodes.description:
Dimension-level sentiment scores from news_trends_dimension_daily_v.

Optionally filter to one cluster (e.g. 'MACRO_SENSITIVITY').
Returns rows with: dimension_key, cluster_id, bucket_day, dim_avg,
                    dim_weighted_avg, article_count.

nodes.label: get_ticker_sentiment
nodes.description:
Per-article per-ticker sentiment from ticker_sentiment_heads_v.

Returns rows with: article_id, ticker, sentiment_score, title, url, published_at.

nodes.label: get_top_articles
nodes.description:
Top-scored articles with full impact vectors.

Returns: title, url, source, published_at, impact_json, top_dimensions, magnitude.

nodes.label: get_ticker_relationships
nodes.description: Graph neighborhood around a ticker via get_relationship_neighborhood RPC.

nodes.label: get_company_vectors
nodes.description: Latest company factor vectors for the given tickers.

nodes.label: get_user_positions
nodes.description:
Net open position per ticker from user_trades.

Returns list of {ticker, net_qty, avg_cost, side}.
net_qty > 0 = long, < 0 = short. avg_cost is weighted average entry price.

nodes.label: get_user_alerts
nodes.description:
Active alerts with latest prices and proximity.

Returns list of {ticker, alert_type, alert_price, direction, notes,
                 latest_price, pct_away}.

nodes.label: get_user_screening_notes
nodes.description:
Active screening tickers from the user's latest scan run.

Returns a flat list of ticker symbols.

nodes.label: search_news
nodes.description:
Semantic search over news articles using vector similarity.

Returns list of {article_id, title, url, published_at, snippet, similarity}.

nodes.label: get_ticker_news
nodes.description:
Per-ticker articles with sentiment scores and relationship annotations.

Uses the get_relationship_node_news RPC (alias resolution + direct mentions +
relationship traceability) then enriches with TICKER_SENTIMENT and
TICKER_RELATIONSHIPS from news_impact_heads.

Returns list of {ticker, article_id, title, url, published_at,
                 sentiment_score, sentiment_reason, relationships}.

nodes.label: count_news_articles_per_calendar_day_utc
nodes.description:
Count stored news rows per UTC calendar day for the last ``n_days`` (inclusive of today).

Each row is bucketed by ``date_trunc('day', COALESCE(published_at, created_at))`` in UTC.
Days with no rows are included with count ``0``.

nodes.label: stock_screener
nodes.description:
FMP stock screener — returns all US-listed stocks that pass basic
liquidity and price filters in a single API call.

Used as an upstream filter before the per-ticker Minervini loop so
that tickers that can't possibly pass are never fetched individually.

Parameters deliberately set slightly looser than our hard thresholds
(price_min=15, volume_min=300k vs our 400k) to avoid edge cases where
FMP's volume metric differs slightly from the quote's avgVolume.

nodes.label: sector_performance
nodes.description: Current daily performance % for each S&P sector.

nodes.label: shares_float
nodes.description: Free float and shares outstanding for a ticker.

nodes.label: institutional_ownership_summary
nodes.description:
Individual institutional holder records from 13-F filings.
Returns one row per holder per reporting date (holder, shares, dateReported, change).
Caller should aggregate by quarter to compute totals and QoQ changes.

nodes.label: income_statement_quarterly

nodes.label: key_metrics_quarterly
nodes.description:
Key fundamental metrics (returnOnEquity, roic, currentRatio, etc.) — last {limit} annual periods.
Note: quarterly period requires a premium FMP subscription; annual is used here.

nodes.label: earnings_calendar_range
nodes.description:
Confirmed upcoming earnings calendar for a date range (all tickers).
from_date, to_date: 'YYYY-MM-DD' strings.
Returns: symbol, date, time (bmo/amc), epsEstimated, revenueEstimated.

Example:
